info.title: SKU.io — Create a Purchase Order
info.description: Create a purchase order in SKU.io — a replenishment order placed with a supplier — from either a plain description of what to buy or a source document (a supplier email, an order-form spreadsheet, a demand-plan export) that supplies the SKUs and quantities. Resolves the supplier, destination warehouse, unit costs, currency, and (optionally) payment term, builds the line items, and creates the PO as a draft or an open order. Covers standard and consignment replenishment. Resolve products with find-product first so every line references a real product.

get.summary: Find a supplier by name/email to get its id (and its default warehouse and payment term).

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by supplier name, email, or company name. Narrow to a single match before using the id.

schema.description: Search by supplier name, email, or company name. Narrow to a single match before using the id.

get.summary: List warehouses (id, name, code, is_default) to resolve the destination warehouse.

List payment terms to resolve payment_term_id. Each term carries an is_consignment boolean (and consignment_settlement_frequency), so the consignment term is identified by that flag rather than by name-guessing. Paginated; there is no search param — page through and match on the returned fields (id, name, is_consignment, is_default, net_days).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number — the list is paginated; iterate if there are more terms than one page.

schema.description: Page number — the list is paginated; iterate if there are more terms than one page.

get.summary: List the account's enabled currencies (id, code, conversion rate, is_default) to resolve or verify currency_code / currency_id.
Most recent unit cost paid for a product on a non-draft PO — fills a line's amount when the source has no cost.

post.summary: Create a purchase order with header fields and an array of line items.

supplier_id.description: The supplier being ordered from (from find-supplier). Required unless you pass supplier_name instead.

supplier_name.description: Supplier name, as an alternative to supplier_id. Prefer resolving supplier_id first; an exact name the API doesn't match will 422.

currency_code.description: ISO currency code for the PO (e.g. "USD", "AUD"). Required unless you pass currency_id. Use the account/supplier base currency; resolve or verify it with list-currencies.

currency_id.description: Currency id, as an alternative to currency_code.

destination_warehouse_id.description: Warehouse the stock is received into (from list-warehouses). Required for a standard PO; omit only for a dropship PO that ships straight to the customer. For a consignment order, this is the consignment (supplier-owned) warehouse.

payment_term_id.description: Payment term for the PO — e.g. the account's consignment term. Optional. Resolve the id with list-payment-terms: for a consignment order pick the term whose is_consignment is true; otherwise match by name or fall back to the supplier's default_payment_term_id. Don't invent it. See INSTRUCTIONS.md.

order_status.description: The only accepted value is "draft" (creates a draft PO). Omit the field entirely to create an open PO.

purchase_order_number.description: Custom PO number. Auto-generated if omitted.

supplier_notes.description: Notes to the supplier, printed on the PO.

submission_format.description: How the PO is sent to the supplier when submitted (e.g. "email").

store_id.description: Store/sales-channel the PO is attributed to.

tags.description: Array of string tags for filtering/reporting.

purchase_order_lines.description: Array of line items. Each line: { product_id OR sku (required unless a pure cost line), quantity (required, >= 0), amount (required unit cost, >= 0), description (recommended), discount, tax_rate_id, estimated_delivery_date, is_free_of_charge all optional }.

financial_lines.description: Optional non-product charges (freight, duties). Each: { description, quantity, amount, financial_line_type_id, allocate_to_products, proration_strategy }.

post.summary: Submit (send to the supplier) one or more created POs. Sends the PO out — confirm first.

ids.description: Array of purchase-order ids to submit to their suppliers.
